songs.title: Y-Y-Yes
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
y… y… y…
(a quick smiling glide)

[Hook 1 — kids chant]
Y! Y! Y-y-y!
What begins with Y?
Y-y-yam! Y-y-yam!
It's a yam! (a yam!)

[Hook 2 — kids chant]
Y! Y! Y-y-y!
What begins with Y?
Y-y-yak! Y-y-yak!
It's a yak! (a yak!)

[Hook 3 — kids chant]
Y! Y! Y-y-y!
What begins with Y?
Y-y-yoyo! Y-y-yoyo!
It's a yoyo! (a yoyo!)

[Breakdown — whispered, bass drops out]
y… y… y…
y-y-yarn… y-y-yacht… y-y-yogurt…

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not Y!
(y… y… y…)

[Final Hook — big]
Y! Y! Y-y-y!
Y-y-yeti! Y-y-yeti!
It's a yeti, yeti, yeti!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The Y sound is a quick smiling glide /y/ that slides straight into the vowel (y-am, y-es), NEVER 'why' (letter name) and NEVER 'yuh' (schwa). Keep it short and springy on the beat. Answer line 'It's a ___!' reuses the Week 1 frame.

songs.title: Yum! A Yam!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(nom… nom… nom…)

[Hook]
Yum! A yam! Yum! A yam!
Is it yum? (yes! yes!)
Yum, yum, a yam!

[Verse 2]
Yum! A nut! Yum! A nut!
Is it yum? (yes! yes!)
Yum, yum, a nut!

[Verse 3]
Yum! A fig! Yum! A fig!
Is it yum? (yes! yes!)
Yum, yum, a fig!

[Verse 4]
A sock?! A sock?!
Is it yum? (NO! NOT yum!)
Not yum, not yum, a sock!

[Outro]
A potato?! Is it yum?
(POTATO! YUM!)
Yum… a yam…
(nom… shhh…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: One frame: 'Yum! A ___!' — the food swaps per verse (yam → nut → fig → sock(!) → potato). Verse 4 is the 'NOT yum' joke (a sock is not food) — that yes/no discrimination is the point, disguised as comedy. Kids choir shouts the (yes! yes!) / (NO! NOT yum!) verdicts.

spreads.artNote: A single roasted orange yam under the spotlight, steam curling up, looking delicious. The anchor word, glowing.
Sam the peg-doll boy taking an enormous happy bite of the yam, cheeks full, eyes shut in bliss.
Someone hopefully offers a mop on a plate. Absolute refusal. The mop looks offended.
The Rat (grey) cracking a nut with both paws, delighted, one cheek already stuffed.
The Duck (white) gulping down a whole purple fig, neck bulging comically, thrilled.
The Dog (brown) licking a spoon of red jam, tail a blur, the happiest dog alive. Week 20 callback.
The Duck with the stripey sock in its bill again, this time confused — is this food? Everyone shakes their heads. Big NO.
An absurdly enormous yam on the mat, the whole cast gathered around it with forks, ready to feast together.
The potato sits on a plate under the spotlight, a fork poised beside it. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS the word from the picture. The potato looks nervous.

extras: Yum / Not-yum sort: put the food pictures (yam, nut, fig, jam) on the YUM plate and the tricksters (sock, wig, rock) on the NOT-YUM plate
Real yam tasting: roast a yam, sound out y-a-m, then vote 'yum? yes!' as a class — the anchor word you can eat
Moveable alphabet: build y-e-s, y-u-m, y-a-m, y-e-t — note y starts words here but will become a sneaky vowel in Level 2

teacherFocus: Teach /y/ as a quick smiling glide that slides into the vowel (y-am, y-es), never 'yuh'. Roast a real yam, sound it out, and vote 'yum? yes!'. Run the yum / not-yum sort — foods on one plate, tricksters (sock, wig, rock) on the other; the yes/no judgement IS the phonics game. Whisper that y will grow up into a sneaky vowel next level.
